product_overview: Forge-api is an HTTP REST wrapper around the Forge autonomous coding agent that allows external tools to trigger single and batch agent runs, run a continuous watcher, and inspect run artifacts (trajectories, stats, issues, health) without invoking the CLI.
core_goals: Trigger single and batch Forge agent runs via HTTP and return structured run results.
Continuously watch a GitHub repository and auto-fix labelled issues via a background watcher.
Expose trajectory files and full agent histories for inspection and debugging.
Aggregate outcome statistics across trajectory directories for reporting and assertions.
List open GitHub issues to discover candidate issues to fix.
Provide a health probe for CI/CD readiness and liveness checks.

features.name: Single Run (POST /api/run)
features.description: Run the Forge agent once on a GitHub issue or a raw problem_text. This is a potentially long-running, blocking endpoint that returns a structured result including exit status, submission preview, model stats, and trajectory filename.
user_flows: POST /api/run with body { "github_url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", "output_dir": "trajectories", "agent": { /* optional overrides */ }, "env": { /* optional overrides */ } } -> Receive 200 with { "exit_status": "submitted", "has_submission": true, "submission_preview": "diff --git a/src/...", "steps": 23, "model_stats": { "total_cost": 0.042 }, "trajectory_file": "trajectories/owner__repo-i42.traj" } -> GET /api/trajectories?dir=trajectories -> Receive 200 with { "directory": "trajectories", "count": n, "trajectories": [ { "name": "owner__repo-i42.traj", "exit_status": "submitted", "has_submission": true, "steps": 23 } ] }
POST /api/run with body missing problem source (no github_url, no repo+issue, no problem_text) -> Receive 400 with error message string

features.name: Batch Run (POST /api/run/batch)
features.description: Run the Forge agent in parallel on multiple inputs (GitHub issues or problem_text items). Returns aggregated success/failure counts and per-item results.
user_flows: POST /api/run/batch with body { "output_dir": "trajectories", "workers": 4, "items": [ { "github_url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/1" }, { "repo": "owner/repo", "issue": 2 }, { "problem_text": "Fix off-by-one" } ] } -> Receive 200 with { "output_dir": "trajectories", "total": 3, "succeeded": 2, "failed": 1, "results": [ { "instance_id": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/1", "success": true, "error": null }, { "instance_id": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/2", "success": true, "error": null }, { "instance_id": "text-Fix the off-by-one", "success": false, "error": "docker not found" } ] } -> GET /api/trajectories?dir=trajectories -> Receive 200 with directory list containing entries for succeeded runs
POST /api/run/batch with malformed body (items missing or not an array) -> Receive 400 with error message string

features.name: Watch Mode (POST/GET/DELETE /api/watch)
features.description: Start a single background GitHub label watcher that polls a repository and runs the agent on new issues with the configured label. Only one watcher may run at a time; endpoints allow start, inspect, and stop.
user_flows: POST /api/watch with body { "repo": "owner/repo", "label": "forge", "interval": 60, "model": "gpt-4o", "api_key": "sk-...", "output_dir": "trajectories" } -> Receive 200 with { "running": true, "repo": "owner/repo", "label": "forge", "started_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z" } -> GET /api/watch -> Receive 200 with { "running": true, "repo": "owner/repo", "label": "forge", "started_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z" } -> DELETE /api/watch -> Receive 200 with { "running": false, "repo": null, "label": null, "started_at": null }
POST /api/watch while a watcher is already running with same or different params -> Receive 409 Conflict with message indicating watch already running

features.name: Trajectory List (GET /api/trajectories)
features.description: List .traj files in a directory along with per-file metadata (exit_status, has_submission, steps, model_stats).
user_flows: GET /api/trajectories?dir=nonexistent_dir -> Receive 404 with directory missing error

features.name: Trajectory Detail (GET /api/trajectories/{name})
features.description: Return the full content of a single .traj file (complete trajectory steps, agent info, environment, and full submission patch).
user_flows: GET /api/trajectories/owner__repo-i42.traj?dir=trajectories -> Receive 200 with full TrajFile JSON containing trajectory steps, info, environment, and submission patch

features.name: Stats (GET /api/stats)
features.description: Aggregate exit_status counts across .traj files in a directory and return totals for submitted, forfeited, errors, step_limit_reached, and other.

features.name: Issue Listing (GET /api/issues)
features.description: List open GitHub issues for an owner/repo (optionally filtered by label and limited). Uses GITHUB_TOKEN when available; may return 502 for invalid repos or rate limit issues without a token.
user_flows: GET /api/issues?repo=owner/repo&label=bug&limit=30 -> Receive 200 with { "repo": "owner/repo", "count": n, "issues": [ { "number": 42, "title": "Fix validation bug", "url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", "labels": ["bug"] } ] } -> POST /api/run with { "github_url": "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/42", "output_dir": "trajectories" } -> Receive 200 with run result
GET /api/issues?repo=invalid/or-missing -> Receive 502 with GitHub API error message

features.name: Health Check (GET /health)
features.description: Liveness/readiness probe returning service status and crate version for CI/CD and uptime checks.
user_flows: GET /health when service is down or unresponsive -> Receive non-200 (service unavailable) or connection error

tech_stack: Docker (via forge-run / bollard for agent runs)

features.name: Forge API HTTP surface
features.description: REST API wrapping the Forge autonomous coding agent (run, batch, GitHub issues, trajectories, stats, watch).

endpoints.description: Liveness probe; returns status ok and crate version
Run Forge agent once on a GitHub issue or problem_text; long-running

response_schema.200: { exit_status, has_submission, submission_preview, steps, model_stats, trajectory_file }

endpoints.description: Parallel agent runs for multiple items
List open GitHub issues for owner/repo; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env when set
List .traj files in dir with metadata
Full TrajFile JSON for one trajectory file
Aggregate exit_status counts over .traj files in directory

response_schema.200: { directory, total, submitted, forfeited, errors, step_limit_reached, other }

endpoints.description: Start background GitHub label watcher (one at a time)

known_limitations.issue: POST /api/run and batch require Docker, model API keys, and can take many minutes
known_limitations.impact: Integration tests should focus on fast endpoints unless environment is fully provisioned

known_limitations.issue: /api/issues returns 502 for invalid repos or rate limits without token
known_limitations.impact: Tests should use a known public repo or skip when GitHub unavailable
